title: Indian Badminton Stars Plan FIFA World Cup Travel Destinations
indian_angle: Top Indian shuttlers like Chirag, Prannoy, Ayush, and Sindhu are making plans to attend the upcoming FIFA World Cup, showing their interests beyond badminton.
summary: Chirag Shetty, HS Prannoy, Ayush Shetty, and PV Sindhu are reportedly planning their travel to various countries like Argentina, Spain, Portugal, and Germany to watch the FIFA World Cup 2026.

title: Grassroot Coaches Development Program Announced For Guwahati And Raipur
indian_angle: This program aims to strengthen the foundation of Indian badminton by training coaches at the grassroot level in key national centers.
summary: The Badminton Association of India announced a Coaches Development Program for the grassroot level to be held in Guwahati and Raipur from June 12-21, 2026, focusing on nurturing future talent.

title: Sindhu, Arjun-Hariharan Advance To Round 2 At Indonesia Open
indian_angle: PV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair Arjun and Hariharan successfully progressed to the second round of the Indonesia Open, keeping India's hopes alive.
summary: PV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair of Arjun M.R. and Hariharan Amsakarunan secured their spots in the second round of the Indonesia Open Super 1000 tournament.

title: India Invites EOI For BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: India is actively preparing to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in 2026, signaling its growing prominence in global badminton.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for the organization of the BWF World Championships 2026, indicating India's bid to host the major international event.

title: Satwik-Chirag Win Singapore Open Title With Furious Rallies And Yorker-Smash
indian_angle: India's top men's doubles pair secured a major BWF Super 750 title, showcasing their dominance on the international stage.
summary: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty claimed the Singapore Open title, displaying aggressive play and unique shots, marking a significant victory for Indian badminton.

title: Lakshya Sen Receives Yellow Card In Singapore Open Loss
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Lakshya Sen faced a challenging match at the Singapore Open, receiving a yellow card during his three-set defeat.
summary: Lakshya Sen lost a hard-fought match at the Singapore Open with scores of 19-21, 21-15, 15-21, and was penalized with a yellow card for throwing his racquet.
